choice_context: Choosing between movies based on thematic elements
reasoning: Identifying bipolar axes that capture the essence of movie preference themes while ensuring distinctiveness and reflecting genuine disagreement among viewers.

dimensions.name: Action vs. Character-Driven

low_pole.label: Action
low_pole.description: Movies with high-stakes action sequences and thrillers.
low_pole.typical_person: Someone who enjoys adrenaline-pumping scenes and fast-paced narratives.

high_pole.label: Character-Driven
high_pole.description: Movies focusing on deep character development and psychological depth.
high_pole.typical_person: Someone who prefers stories that explore complex personalities and emotional journeys.

dimensions.scoring_guidance: Score higher for movies with intense action and lower for those with intricate character arcs.

dimensions.name: Historical vs. Contemporary

low_pole.label: Historical
low_pole.description: Movies set in historical periods or biographical content.
low_pole.typical_person: Someone interested in past eras and factual storytelling.

high_pole.label: Contemporary
high_pole.description: Movies set in modern times or dealing with current issues.
high_pole.typical_person: Someone who prefers stories relevant to today’s society.

dimensions.scoring_guidance: Score higher for movies set in historical contexts and lower for contemporary settings.

dimensions.name: Romantic vs. Non-Romantic

low_pole.label: Non-Romantic
low_pole.description: Movies without romantic elements or focus.
low_pole.typical_person: Someone who prefers stories that do not center around love.

high_pole.label: Romantic
high_pole.description: Movies with strong romantic dramas and emotional journeys.
high_pole.typical_person: Someone who enjoys stories about love and relationships.

dimensions.scoring_guidance: Score higher for movies with significant romantic storylines and lower for those without.

dimensions.name: Family-Friendly vs. Adult-Themed

low_pole.label: Adult-Themed
low_pole.description: Movies with mature themes and content.
low_pole.typical_person: Someone who prefers films with complex, adult-oriented narratives.

high_pole.label: Family-Friendly
high_pole.description: Movies suitable for all ages, often animated.
high_pole.typical_person: Someone who enjoys films that can be watched with children.

dimensions.scoring_guidance: Score higher for family-friendly content and lower for adult-themed movies.

dimensions.name: Visual Appeal vs. Narrative Complexity

low_pole.label: Narrative Complexity
low_pole.description: Movies with intricate plots and themes.
low_pole.typical_person: Someone who appreciates layered storytelling and intellectual engagement.

high_pole.label: Visual Appeal
high_pole.description: Movies with stunning visuals and cinematography.
high_pole.typical_person: Someone who values the aesthetic and visual aspects of a film.

dimensions.scoring_guidance: Score higher for visually appealing movies and lower for those with complex narratives.

dimensions.name: Humor vs. Seriousness

low_pole.label: Seriousness
low_pole.description: Movies with serious tones and themes.
low_pole.typical_person: Someone who prefers films that tackle heavy subjects.

high_pole.label: Humor
high_pole.description: Movies with comedic elements and light-hearted stories.
high_pole.typical_person: Someone who enjoys films that make them laugh.

dimensions.scoring_guidance: Score higher for humorous movies and lower for serious ones.

dimensions.name: Classic Adaptation vs. Original Story

low_pole.label: Original Story
low_pole.typical_person: Someone who prefers fresh, new stories.

high_pole.label: Classic Adaptation
high_pole.description: Movies adapted from classic literature or well-known works.
high_pole.typical_person: Someone who enjoys seeing familiar stories brought to life.

dimensions.scoring_guidance: Score higher for adaptations of classics and lower for original stories.

dimensions.name: Realism vs. Fantasy

low_pole.label: Fantasy
low_pole.description: Movies with supernatural or fantastical elements.
low_pole.typical_person: Someone who enjoys imaginative and magical stories.

high_pole.label: Realism
high_pole.description: Movies grounded in reality with social commentary.
high_pole.typical_person: Someone who prefers films that reflect real-world issues.

dimensions.scoring_guidance: Score higher for realistic movies and lower for those with fantasy elements.

dimensions.name: Star Power vs. Ensemble Cast

low_pole.label: Ensemble Cast
low_pole.typical_person: Someone who enjoys films with a variety of characters and performances.

high_pole.label: Star Power
high_pole.description: Movies starring famous actors or actresses.
high_pole.typical_person: Someone who prefers films with notable star performances.

dimensions.scoring_guidance: Score higher for movies with star power and lower for ensemble casts.

dimensions.name: Adventure vs. Drama

low_pole.label: Drama
low_pole.description: Movies focused on dramatic storytelling and character development.
low_pole.typical_person: Someone who enjoys emotionally rich and character-focused narratives.

high_pole.label: Adventure
high_pole.description: Movies with adventurous and survival themes.
high_pole.typical_person: Someone who prefers films with exciting adventures and quests.

dimensions.scoring_guidance: Score higher for adventure movies and lower for dramatic ones.

redundancy_check.correlation_note: Both dimensions involve action and adventure themes, but Dimension 1 focuses more on character development versus action, while Dimension 10 contrasts adventure with drama.
redundancy_check.disentanglement_suggestion: Maintain both dimensions as they address different aspects of narrative focus.

redundancy_check.correlation_note: Both dimensions deal with emotional and thematic content, but Dimension 3 specifically addresses romantic elements, whereas Dimension 6 covers humor versus seriousness.
redundancy_check.disentanglement_suggestion: Keep both dimensions as they cover distinct emotional and thematic areas.
